$schema: TestingBurndown — manual test items for the MeaningWave Phase 1 work. Each item is a change the user needs to verify. Flip `status` to `passed` or `failed` after manual testing. New entries are appended at the bottom.

items.title: Module repacks cleanly with new scripts
steps: Run `nwn-manager repack`.
Verify no compile errors for any mw_* script.
items.expectedResult: Module packs to dist/ without errors.
items.notes: Include compile only fails when a consumer script compiles — if you see an error in mw_pet_unl.nss or mw_on_rest.nss complaining about MW_Unlock / MW_UnlockCount, the mw_unlock_inc.nss include itself has the bug.

items.title: Rest opens emotewand cleanly (no MW menu when no unlocks)
steps: Start a fresh PC with no unlocks.
Rest. Verify the legacy emotewand conversation opens exactly as before — no MeaningWave-flavoured menu replaces it.
items.expectedResult: Resting opens the legacy emotewand conversation. No regression versus pre-change.
items.notes: Mod_OnPlrRest is restored to its original 'on_mod_rest'. MeaningWave options live inside emotewand and are conditionally hidden when MW_UnlockCount == 0.

items.title: [OBSOLETE] mw_rest_menu dialogue (replaced by emotewand submenu)
steps: Travel to Rivendell Upper Halls.
Walk to coordinates roughly (30, 80) — opposite side of the area from Elrond.
Verify a male human named 'Jordan Peterson' is standing there, hostile-immune (Plot=1).
Click to converse — should open the meet-dialogue prompt 'Stand up straight, with your shoulders back…'
items.expectedResult: Peterson is reachable and his dialogue starts.
items.notes: Replaced by the emotewand integration. See MW-028 for the new test.

items.title: Peterson quiz: pass path unlocks the guide
steps: Talk to Peterson, pick 'Test me.'

Answer correctly on at least 4 of 5: Q1='nothing makes you stronger…', Q2='The path to meaning, freely chosen.', Q3='Hierarchies of competence…', Q4='The one in my own cellar…', Q5='True — but the order is never finished.'
Verify Peterson's pass message appears.
Verify floating text 'You have gained the wisdom of Jordan Peterson…' appears.
Verify 500 XP awarded.
Open journal — `MW: Jordan Peterson` should show stage 2 (complete), `The Path of Meaning` should show stage 2.
items.expectedResult: Pass branch fires, MW_Unlock runs, journal updates, XP and VFX awarded.
items.notes: If unlock floating text appears but journal doesn't update, check that the journal tags exactly match: 'MW Jordan Peterson' and 'MW Path of Meaning' (case-sensitive).

items.title: Peterson quiz: fail path lets you retry
steps: Talk to Peterson, pick 'Test me.', answer wrong on 2+ of the 5 questions.
Verify the fail entry plays ('You stumble…').
Talk to Peterson again — quiz should reset and let you retry.
items.expectedResult: Failing the quiz doesn't lock you out. Retry works because mw_pet_reset is called on 'Test me.' reply.
items.notes: If the score persists across retries, mw_pet_reset isn't firing — verify it's set as the Reply Script on the accept reply, not on the Entry.

items.title: [OBSOLETE] rest menu listing (replaced by emotewand submenu)
steps: After MW-004 (Peterson unlocked), rest anywhere safe.
Verify the menu 'A familiar music whispers…' appears with: 'Jordan Peterson — Sage of Order.', '(No guide — just rest.)', '(Open the emote wand instead.)'.
Locked guides (Watts, Campbell, etc.) should NOT appear as options.
items.expectedResult: Only the unlocked guide is offered; fallbacks present.
items.notes: See MW-028.

items.title: Selecting Peterson from rest menu summons him as a henchman
steps: From the rest menu (MW-006), pick 'Jordan Peterson — Sage of Order.'.
Verify a creature spawns at the PC's location, joins the party as a henchman, and follows the PC.
Walk to a new area — Peterson should appear in that area near the PC (mw_hench_hb heartbeat-follow).
Talk to Peterson — should open the henchman chat with 'What is it? Speak plainly.', 3 reply options.
items.expectedResult: Peterson is a working henchman.
items.notes: If 'blueprint mw_peterson missing' floating text appears, the .utc.json wasn't packed (check nasher's source list).

items.title: Summoning a second guide dismisses the first
steps: Unlock Peterson via the quiz (MW-004).
Unlock at least one other guide (e.g. Watts in Old Forest at (60,60) — same quiz pattern).
Rest, summon Peterson. Then rest again, summon Watts.
Verify Peterson EffectDisappears and Watts spawns. Only one mw_* guide should be active.
items.expectedResult: Only one MeaningWave guide is active at a time.
items.notes: MW_DismissActiveGuide iterates henchmen and dismisses any with tag prefix 'mw_'.

items.title: Henchman dismissal from chat
steps: With Peterson summoned, talk to him, pick 'Walk apart from me for now, Jordan.'
Verify Peterson disappears (calls hench_kill action script).
items.expectedResult: Standard henchman-dismiss behaviour works.

items.title: Wayshrine of the Wave in the Well of Eru
steps: Travel to thewelloferu (module entry point).
Find the Wayshrine of the Wave near (10, 10).
Click it with a PC who has no unlocks — should show floating text 'The obelisk hums, but the music is too distant.'
Click it with a PC who has unlocked Peterson — should teleport to Hall of Legends.
items.expectedResult: Wayshrine gates entry on having ≥1 unlock.
items.notes: If the Wayshrine doesn't appear, the placement in thewelloferu.git.json didn't take. If clicking does nothing, the 'Useable' byte may have been overridden — re-check the placed instance.

items.title: Hall of Legends loads and swaps statues for live NPCs
steps: Teleport via the Wayshrine to the Hall.
Verify the area loads (small 2x2 wizard-tower interior, re-skinned).
Verify Akira the Don is present near the centre (8, 8).

Verify 7 statues are arranged in a ring around him — except the Peterson pedestal, which should now show the live Peterson world NPC instead of a statue (because the entering PC has Peterson unlocked).
Walk to where 'Peterson' stands in the Hall — should be able to talk to him.
items.expectedResult: Statue→live-NPC swap fires for unlocked guides on Hall entry. Other 6 pedestals still hold statues.
items.notes: The cloned wizard-tower tileset will not visually look like a 'Hall of Legends'. This is intentional Phase-1 scaffolding — the area needs toolset re-skinning to feel right. See MW-014.

items.title: Peterson's Hall NPC has its own conversation slot
steps: Currently both the Rivendell world-instance and the Hall instance of Peterson share the same dialogue (mw_peterson_m — the unlock quiz). After unlock, talking to Hall-Peterson re-presents the quiz, which is jarring.

Author mw_peterson_l.dlg.json (Hall-specific dialogue) and either: (a) override Conversation on the in-Hall instance to mw_peterson_l, or (b) have mw_peterson_m branch on MW_IsUnlocked to show post-unlock lore.
items.expectedResult: Hall-Peterson speaks lore/loyalty content, not the quiz.
items.notes: Ships in task 6/7 — flagged here so user knows the current behaviour.

items.title: Akira the Don is reachable but currently has no dialogue
steps: Akira's UTC sets Conversation = 'mw_akira', but mw_akira.dlg.json hasn't been created yet (ships in task 6).
Clicking Akira in-game will fail to open a dialogue.
items.expectedResult: Once mw_akira.dlg.json ships, clicking Akira opens his curator dialogue.
items.notes: Phase-1 cosmetic gap; Akira's full lore + finale gating ships in task 6.

items.title: Hall of Legends visual polish
steps: The Hall is currently a 2x2 tile clone of the wizard tower (tin01 tileset). It functions but looks like a wizard's chamber, not a grand hall.
In the NWN:EE toolset: open the module, re-tile or re-skin the area, add proper lighting, paint pedestals, add ambient effects.
Round-trip back to JSON via nwn_gff.
items.expectedResult: Hall feels like a hall.
items.notes: Optional polish; gameplay is unaffected.

items.title: Watts in Old Forest — quiz unlock
steps: Travel to Old Forest. Find Alan Watts at (60, 60).
Engage. Pick 'Ask. I will answer.'
Correct answers: 'There is no separate I…', 'A game played in hide-and-seek…', 'Because the trying is the play…', 'A verb — selving.', 'By falling on purpose, with grace.'
Pass branch should unlock Watts; journal 'MW: Alan Watts' completes; 'Path of Meaning' ticks up.
items.expectedResult: Watts unlocks and becomes summonable.
items.notes: Same quiz/dialogue mechanics as Peterson — if Watts fails but Peterson worked, look at mw_wat_pass.nss for typo.

items.title: Campbell in Balin's Tomb — quiz unlock
steps: Travel to Balin's Tomb. Find Joseph Campbell at (12, 12).
Correct answers: 'The Call to Adventure.', 'To test whether you are worthy of crossing.', 'To die to what they were…', 'Following the deepest urge in you…', 'A boon for the village.'
items.expectedResult: Campbell unlocks.
items.notes: Plan called for a fetch-quest unlock for Campbell; Phase-1 simplification uses the shared quiz pattern. Quest-style unlock is captured in MW-021.

items.title: McKenna in Northern Forests of Ithilien — quiz unlock
steps: Travel to Northern Forests of Ithilien. Find Terence McKenna at (90, 90).
Correct answers: 'Your operating system.', 'To remember the felt presence…', 'A fractal of novelty intensifying.', '…is closer to you than your own breath.', 'An organism that wants to be eaten…'
items.expectedResult: McKenna unlocks.

items.title: Jocko in Helm's Deep — quiz unlock
steps: Travel to Helm's Deep. Find Jocko Willink at (80, 30).
Correct answers: 'Freedom.', 'Mine. Extreme ownership.', 'Good. We will adapt and continue.', 'Up. Now.', 'Do it anyway…'
items.expectedResult: Jocko unlocks. GOOD.
items.notes: Plan called for a combat trial. Phase-1 uses the shared quiz pattern; quest-style unlock captured in MW-022.

items.title: Jung in Esgaroth Crypts — quiz unlock
steps: Travel to Esgaroth Crypts. Find Carl Jung at (25, 25).
Correct answers: 'The disowned parts…', 'By integrating the shadow.', 'Becoming the totality…', 'The collective inheritance…', '…the mask you wear; useful, but not you.'
items.expectedResult: Jung unlocks.
items.notes: Plan called for a shadow-doppelganger combat. Quest-style unlock captured in MW-023.

items.title: Aurelius at Gwathdor Throne — quiz unlock
steps: Travel to Gwathdor: Throne of the Lord. Find Marcus Aurelius at (40, 20).
Correct answers: 'Your judgements, intentions, and reactions…', '…the way.', 'Remember that you will die.', 'The colour of its thoughts.', 'You will meet the ungrateful…'
items.expectedResult: Aurelius unlocks.

items.title: Quest-style unlocks (Campbell, McKenna, Jocko, Jung)
steps: Plan called for Campbell = mini hero's-journey quest, McKenna = mushroom-vision encounter, Jocko = combat trial, Jung = shadow-fight.
Phase 1 uses the shared 5-question quiz pattern for all 7 to keep the system uniform.
Replacing these later: leave the unlock plumbing (`MW_Unlock(oPC, '<g>')`) and swap the meet dialogue / add triggers + scripts that call `MW_Unlock` on quest completion.
items.expectedResult: Optional polish — gameplay works without it.
items.notes: All 7 unlocks currently funnel through MW_Unlock, so the unlock pathway is uniform. Replacing the quiz with a quest is a per-guide swap that doesn't touch the rest/Hall/summon system.

items.title: Resref sanity: nasher picks up all 73 new files
steps: After `nwn-manager repack`, verify dist/<name>.mod was built.
Count new mw_* files in the .mod via toolset if doubtful — 15 UTC + 1 UTP + 15 DLG + 48 NSS = 79 mw-prefixed assets.
If any of the mw_* files were skipped, check nasher.cfg's source glob to ensure `unpacked/mw_*` is included.
items.expectedResult: All mw_* assets are in the packed module.

items.title: Akira's curator dialogue works (no finale yet)
steps: Reach the Hall of Legends with at least 1 unlock (via Wayshrine).
Click Akira the Don near the centre.
Verify the greeting plays and 3 reply options appear: 'Show me my progress.', 'Tell me about the Hall.', 'Farewell, Don.'
The 'Give me the mixtape.' reply should NOT appear with <7 unlocks; the 'Thank you for the mixtape.' reply should NOT appear (no finale yet).
items.expectedResult: Akira responds; gated replies are hidden.

items.title: Finale: receive the Mixtape when all 7 are unlocked
steps: Unlock all 7 guides (do all the quizzes, or DM-set the campaign ints).
Verify the 'Path of Meaning' journal advances to stage 8 ('All seven legends are mine. Akira awaits…').
Travel to Hall, talk to Akira.
Verify 'I have gathered all seven. Give me the mixtape.' reply appears.
Pick it. Verify: VFX plays, +5000 XP, mw_mixtape item appears in inventory, journal advances to stage 9 (End=1).
Equip the Mixtape (amulet slot). Verify +2 to all 6 ability scores.
Talk to Akira again. Verify the finale reply is gone; the 'Thank you for the mixtape.' reply now appears.
items.expectedResult: Finale fires once, mixtape granted, meta-quest closed.
items.notes: If 'Give me the mixtape' shows up before unlocking 7 guides, mw_finale_chk isn't gating properly.

items.title: Hall NPCs use the Hall dialogue, not the unlock quiz
steps: Unlock at least one guide.
Travel to Hall (Wayshrine).
Click the live guide NPC (e.g. Peterson) on the pedestal.
Verify the Hall greeting plays ('Welcome, friend. Stand up straight…' for Peterson), not the quiz intro.
items.expectedResult: Hall guides give a short post-unlock greeting; quiz intro only appears in the world location (Rivendell etc.).

items.title: Loyalty quest scaffolds exist as journal categories
steps: After repack, open journal — 7 'MW Loyalty: <name>' categories should be present but have no entries until they're triggered.
Real content (quest steps, completion scripts, stat-bump payoff) is not yet implemented.
items.expectedResult: Categories exist; entries empty until Phase-2/polish ships them.
items.notes: Plan called for full loyalty quests with stat-bump payoff on completion. Phase 1 ships the scaffolding; the actual gameplay content is captured here as a deferred item.

items.title: Phase 2 — music HAK workflow (deferred)
steps: Phase 2 work: create homerwave.hak with custom ambientmusic.2da + 9 .bmu files.
Add 'homerwave' to module.ifo.json Mod_HakList.
Set hallofleg's MusicDay/MusicNight to new track row IDs.
Optionally swap music per guide area + on summon.
items.expectedResult: Custom music plays in the Hall and per-guide areas.
items.notes: All Phase-1 code paths are music-agnostic; the HAK is purely additive.

items.title: emotewand submenu: 'Summon a Legend' appears only when ≥1 guide is unlocked
steps: Start a fresh PC. Rest (or use the emote wand item directly).
Verify the existing 7 top-level options are unchanged: Admin Options, Teleports, Force Rest, Role Dice, Summons Options, Options of the Homeless, Exit.
Verify the new '[Summon a Legend.]' option is HIDDEN (no guides unlocked yet).
Unlock at least one guide (e.g. talk to Peterson in Rivendell Upper Halls and pass the quiz, or DM-console SetCampaignInt('meaningwave','u_peterson',1,oPC)).
Rest (or activate the wand) again.
Verify '[Summon a Legend.]' now APPEARS at the bottom of the option list.
Pick it. Submenu shows: only Peterson visible (other 6 guides hidden), plus '(Back.)'.
Pick Peterson. Verify he spawns as henchman and the conversation ends.
Re-open emote wand: pick Summon a Legend → Back. Should return to top-level options.
items.expectedResult: Top-level 'Summon a Legend' visible iff MW_UnlockCount >= 1. Submenu only shows unlocked guides. Picking summons; back returns.
items.notes: Files removed in the integration: mw_on_rest.nss, mw_rest_menu.dlg.json, mw_emote_open.nss. The rest hook is reverted to the original on_mod_rest.

items.title: emotewand integration leaves existing options intact
steps: Open emote wand.
Try each of the 7 original options (Teleports submenu, Force Rest, Role Dice, etc.) — verify each still works as before.
Test as a DM-flag holder that admin/summon submenus still appear correctly.
items.expectedResult: No regression in any pre-existing emotewand path.
items.notes: We only added a new reply to entry 0 and a new submenu entry; no existing replies/entries were modified.
